title: Historical Credentialed Mutation-Seam Hosted Repair And Public PM Live-Data Proof With Custom-Domain DNS Pending
historical_note: This record preserves one earlier Dev Residency transition slice from before the canonical repo boundary moved to C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform on 2026-05-07. It is historical provenance, not a live mutation-seam or AI-boundary transition surface for current repo operations.
current_routing: use PROJECT_STATUS.md for the current residue-retirement lane and latest completed packets
use docs/architecture/OLARES-PUBLICATION-BOUNDARY-RETIREMENT-DEPENDENCY-INVENTORY-2026-05-06.md for the remaining post-cutover boundary closeout queue
use this record only when historical provenance is needed for the earlier Dev Residency 033 transition slice preserved here
objective: Use the authenticated Render surface plus direct hosted-database repair to close the functional public PM hosted lane by bringing mutation-seam live on Render's default hostname, temporarily pointing `apex-operations-web` production at that live host, and rerunning governed public proof while preserving truthful custom-domain DNS residue.
decision_rationale: Once browser authorization was available, the highest-value next slice was to complete the credentialed hosted lane rather than wait on a separate operator because both Render service configuration and live runtime inspection became executable from this environment.

The newly created Render service exposed two real runtime blockers after root-directory correction: missing `psycopg2` in the deployed environment and unconditional demo seeding against the persisted Postgres runtime. Those source fixes were already published on `clean-main` in commits `7ddfd42` and `8669f02`, so the next truthful step was hosted verification rather than more source churn.

The Render default hostname became healthy before the intended custom domain existed publicly, so the smallest route to real public PM proof was to validate the live onrender host, repoint `apex-operations-web` production at that host, and rerun the governed promoted-host proof while keeping `mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com` DNS as explicit residue rather than blocking the functional lane.

Direct runtime proof showed the hosted database still lacked the `schedule` schema and the `seam` tables the mutation-seam store expects, so a bounded hosted-database repair was necessary to convert the healthy process into a healthy PM runtime.
execution_summary: Attached `mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com` to the Render service and captured the required DNS target `mutation-seam -> apex-platform-mutation-seam.onrender.com`, leaving Render in `Waiting for DNS` until GoDaddy is updated.

Validated that `https://apex-platform-mutation-seam.onrender.com` was the live mutation-seam runtime host, then inspected Render logs to identify the real hosted failure: `schedule.projects` did not exist in the Supabase-backed runtime database.

Connected directly to the hosted Supabase database, created the missing minimal `seam` schema tables required by the persisted store, applied the repo-owned `schedule` migrations, and loaded the schedule fixture data through `run_schedule_bootstrap.py` until `schedule.projects`, `schedule.tasks`, and related rows existed for live reads.
Revalidated the live Render host with `apps/mutation-seam/scripts/smoke_deployed_mutation_seam.py` and closed it green at the default Render hostname after the hosted database repair.

Repointed `MUTATION_SEAM_BASE_URL` on the real Vercel project `apex-operations-web` from the unresolved custom domain to `https://apex-platform-mutation-seam.onrender.com`, then cut production to the newest ready preview-derived deployment so the public alias served the ingress-enabled build with the live seam target.

Verified `https://operations.apexpowerops.com/api/v1/reads/approval-queue`, `/api/v1/schedule/projects`, `/api/v1/schedule/drivers`, `/api/v1/schedule/tracer?task_id=probe-task`, and `/api/v1/schedule/variance` all return `200 application/json`, then reran the governed promoted-host proof to `PROMOTED_HOST_SUMMARY failed=0`.

validation.render_custom_domain_attachment: Render service settings -> PASS (`mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com` attached; Render requested CNAME `mutation-seam` -> `apex-platform-mutation-seam.onrender.com`; verification remains `Waiting for DNS`)
validation.hosted_db_repair_proof: direct hosted Supabase query -> PASS (`schedule.projects=1`, `schedule.tasks=4`, `schedule.relationships=3`, `seam.tasks=0`, `seam.workpackages=0` after schema creation and fixture load)
validation.production_cutover: `npx vercel promote https://apex-operations-c1yly5d5z-jasonlswenson-sys-projects.vercel.app --yes` -> PASS (created production deployment `dpl_CdxiFmzZ9q9ASirexMg5GeqTZ3Qn`, aliased to `https://operations.apexpowerops.com`)
validation.public_same_origin_routes: direct probes against production alias -> PASS (`/api/v1/reads/approval-queue`, `/api/v1/schedule/projects`, `/api/v1/schedule/drivers`, `/api/v1/schedule/tracer?task_id=probe-task`, `/api/v1/schedule/variance` all returned `200 application/json`)
validation.promoted_host_proof: `npx pnpm --dir C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform --filter @apex/operations-web smoke:promoted-host --operations-web-base-url https://operations.apexpowerops.com --control-plane-base-url https://control.apexpowerops.com --skip-authenticated-checks` -> PASS (`PROMOTED_HOST_SUMMARY failed=0`)

next_packet_candidate: Olares Dev Residency 034 - Mutation-Seam Custom-Domain DNS Closure And Operations-Web Env Realignment
closure_note: Executed 2026-05-06 as the credentialed hosted follow-through packet. Packet 033 truthfully closes the functional PM hosted lane through the live Render hostname and production browser-host proof, but it does not claim that `https://mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com` already resolves publicly or that production has been switched back to the custom-domain seam target.
next_action: Add the GoDaddy CNAME `mutation-seam -> apex-platform-mutation-seam.onrender.com`, wait for Render verification, restage `MUTATION_SEAM_BASE_URL=https://mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com` on `apex-operations-web`, perform one final server-side production cutover, and rerun the public PM ingress probes against the custom-domain target.
